data.title: Mexico's central bank cuts interest rate by 25 basis points to 7.50%
data.description:
The board of governors of Mexico's central bank on Thursday voted to cut its benchmark overnight lending rate by 25 basis points to 7.50%, in line with market expectations. Banxico, as the bank is known, cited sluggishness in economic activity in the third quarter and significant downside risks from trade tensions for its move.

Latin America's second-biggest economy has been struggling with sticky inflation and a tumultuous tariff relationship with the United States.

Latest government data showed annual headline inflation increasing to 3.74% in the third quarter, from 3.51%. Core inflation also ticked up to 4.26% from 4.23%. Meanwhile, the International Monetary Fund projects annual real GDP growth of only 0.2% in 2025 for Mexico, down from 2024's 1.4%.       

"The Governing Board deemed appropriate to continue the rate-cutting cycle. This decision was consistent with the assessment of the current inflationary outlook. In particular, it took into account the behavior of the exchange rate, the weakness of economic activity, and the possible impact of changes in trade policies worldwide. It also considered the level of monetary restriction that has been implemented," Banxico said in a statement. 

The decision to cut rates by 25 basis points was not a unanimous one, Banxico said. Of the five members on the bank's board, Jonathan Heath voted in favor of keeping the key rate unchanged at 7.75%.  

"Looking ahead, the Board will assess further adjustments to the reference rate. It will take into account the effects of all determinants of inflation. Actions will be implemented in such a way that the reference rate remains consistent at all times with the trajectory needed to enable an orderly and sustained convergence of headline inflation to the 3% target during the forecast period," Banxico said.

"The central bank reaffirms its commitment to its primary mandate and the need to continue its efforts to consolidate an environment of low and stable inflation," it added.

Here are some Mexico-linked exchange-traded funds of interest: (NYSEARCA:EWW), FLMX), and (NYSEARCA:MEXX).

data.title: Wall Street ended in the red after jobless claims fell and Q2 GDP was revised higher
data.description:
Wall Street finished lower in a roller coaster session on Thursday as the broader market averages observed selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) had dropped 0.5%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the red by 0.5% as well and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished downward by 0.3%. 

Across sectors, nine of the 11 S&P groups concluded in negative trading territory, with Health Care suffering the most. At the other end, Energy was the strongest performing segment on the session as oil (CL1:COM) climbed 0.7%. 

U.S. Treasury yields also moved higher. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 3 basis points to 4.18% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.66%. A 7-year note auction tailed, indicating weaker demand for government-backed fixed-income securities.

At the same time the dollar index (DXY) tagged a 3-week high.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims dropped for the second week.

Q2 GDP was revised higher to +3.8% in the third estimate, intensifying the rebound.

Durable goods orders surprised higher in August, while the U.S. international goods trade deficit narrowed more than expected in August.

"Real GDP numbers were good, jobless claims were decent, and we didn’t see any major bad news in other areas. To me, stock weakness is just the result of some profit-taking. After a few extremely strong months, the market leaders have become quite richly valued, making the market prone to days like today. I wouldn’t read too much into it,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

“With regard to tomorrow’s PCE numbers, unless we get a substantially higher number, I doubt the market will start to price in fewer Fed rate cuts,” Nelissen added.

“Thursday's upward GDP revision for second quarter confirmed that the economy grew at a healthy clip, even as tariff uncertainty reached fever pitch during the quarter. The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

“Thursday's GDP strength likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected path of rate cuts, since the data is backward looking and was already well-known. The Fed wants to lower rates to keep the labor market in check and is able to do so at this time since inflation has remained under control, even though it's still above the Fed's target,” Stanley added.

Furthermore, the Fed balance sheet will come after the close.

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, all took the stage at different events.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Intel (INTC) advanced on the session by 8.8%, while Oracle (ORCL) declined by 5.5%.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan downgraded at Scotiabank, but Bernstein says worst is over in upgrade
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) -6.4% in Thursday's trading, down as much as 22% in two days after the copper miner warned of substantial near-term production deferrals following the deadly mud rush at its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia on September 8.

In response, Scotiabank analyst Orest Wowkadaw said the company's update is "very negative," downgrading shares to Sector Perform from Sector Outperform with a $45 price target, lowered from $55, anticipating a significant overhang until better clarity arrives on the full impact of this event.

The incident occurred in one of five production blocks in the GBC and damaged infrastructure required to support other areas of the mine, with preliminary assessments indicating the impacts likely will result in the deferral of significant near-term production due to required repairs and a phased restart, with a return to normal operating rates potentially in 2027.

As a result, Scotiabank's estimated Freeport's (NYSE:FCX) 2025-26 EBITDA estimates declined by an average of 27%/year and 8% NAV/share estimate shed 10% based on preliminary guidance, and no longer forecasts a positive free cash flow inflection or a meaningful pickup in the buyback program in 2026.

But Bernstein analyst Bob Brackett said investors have "overly punished" Freeport (NYSE:FCX), upgrading shares to Outperform from Market Perform with a $48.50 price target, saying the stock's relative performance outweighs the Grasberg recovery risk.

data.title: Scilex to make $150M bitcoin investment in Datavault AI
data.description:
Scilex Holding (NASDAQ:SCLX) announced on Thursday an agreement to make a $150M bitcoin investment in Datavault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT).

The investment is intended to provide growth capital to Datavault to accelerate its supercomputing infrastructure, expand independent data exchanges, and unlock new revenue streams.

Scilex is expected to receive up to 278,914,094 Datavault shares (at an effective purchase price of $0.5378 per share).

Scilex will have the right to nominate two directors to Datavault's board for so long as Scilex maintains beneficial ownership of at least 10%.

There will be the right to nominate one director for so long as Scilex maintains beneficial ownership of at least 5%.

data.title: Fed's Daly says 'a little bit more' rate cutting will be appropriate over time
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco Mary Daly said Thursday that "a little bit more" interest-rate cutting "will be needed over time," noting the inflation impact of tariffs have not been as large as expected, while the impact on growth and employment have been softer than expected.

"Interest rates remain modestly restrictive," Daly said in a fireside chat at a San Francisco Fed event. That will continue to bring inflation down, but  not so restrictive that they unnecessarily bridle and ultimately injur the labor market."

Daly said Wednesday that she "fully supported" the Federal Open Market Committee's decision last week to cut interest rates and that further monetary policy easing will probably be needed going forward.

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

data.title: Netflix to exclusively stream MLB's Yankees vs. Giants season opener in 2026 - report
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) will stream Major League Baseball’s Opening Day game between the New York Yankees and San Francisco Giants next year, according to a Thursday report by The Athletic, citing sources.

The report said as part of the new three-year agreement between the MLB and the streaming giant, the opening game at San Francisco will be Netflix’s first exclusive stream and will kick off the 2026 season.

The announcement is expected to be made in the coming weeks.

The baseball league divided ESPN’s old package of Sunday Night Baseball, the Home Run Derby, and the playoffs between Netflix and NBC/Peacock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) after the Disney-owned sports network (DIS) chose to stay out of the final three years of a deal in which it was set to pay $550M on average.

Sources told the sports news outlet the exact figures for the MLB-Netflix and MLB-NBC/Peacock three-year deals are not yet known but are expected to be in the $225M to $250M per season range.

data.title: Cisco shows off new software to connect quantum computing cloud
data.description:
Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) announced new software on Thursday that is aimed at connecting a quantum computing cloud.

The software prototypes, which will become live next week at Cisco's Quantum Summit, include: a quantum compiler, which enables quantum algorithms to run across multiple networked processors; a quantum alert, which provides security with guarantees from physics; and a quantum sync, a decision coordination application demo that uses entanglement to enable correlated decision-making across distributed locations for classical use cases.

“At Cisco, we have always believed in a full-stack approach to building robust, high-performing systems,” Cisco executive Vijoy Pandey wrote in a blog post. “For classical networking, we design and build this stack from the ground up—custom silicon, integrated hardware systems, and all the layers of software that control and manage everything. It’s the philosophy that enabled us to pioneer many of the foundational technologies of the classical internet, from the core of the data center to the edge of wireless networks, and all the connectivity in between.”

Cisco shares rose fractionally in late day trading on Thursday.

data.title: FDA removes REMS program for Sanofi thyroid cancer drug Caprelsa
data.description:
• The FDA has withdrawn the Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies (REMS) program for Sanofi's (NASDAQ:SNY) medullary thyroid cancer treatment Caprelsa (vandetanib).
  • The agency said that since the drug was approved in 2011, there have been no reports of heart rhythm issues or unexplained sudden deaths associated with the med in US patients.
  • The FDA added that clinical data also found no patterns indicating heart rhythm problems. 
  • Caprelsa was developed by Genzyme, which was acquired by Sanofi in 2011 for $20.1B.

data.title: RFK Jr. reportedly weighs listing autism for vaccine compensation
data.description:
Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is considering plans to add certain autism symptoms to a list of adverse events that require his department to compensate under the national vaccine injury program, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing an advisor.

As HHS Secretary, Kennedy, a well-known vaccine skeptic, has the authority to determine which injuries should be compensated under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program.

Established in the 1980s, the program maintains a fund to offer compensation for people who experience injuries from covered vaccines. It was implemented after lawsuits related to vaccine injury claims jeopardized U.S. vaccine availability and threatened the country’s immunization rates.

On Monday, announcing a range of measures to address autism in children, President Donald Trump, flanked by RFK Jr. and Medicare Administrator Mehmet Oz, suggested a link between the neurodevelopmental disorder and vaccines.

"There are certain groups of people that don't take vaccines and don't take any pills that have no autism," Trump said, triggering backlash from the medical community over his efforts to peddle claims widely debunked in scientific studies.

Leading vaccine makers: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/ BioNTech (BNTX), Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Novavax (NVAX), GSK (NYSE:GSK), Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Lithium Americas extends big gains despite TD Cowen downgrade
data.description:
Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC) +18.1% in Thursday's trading to a two-year intraday high $7.53, adding to its 95% surge in the previous session following reports that the Trump administration is seeking an equity stake in the company as it renegotiates terms of a $2.3 billion U.S. government loan.

The dizzying gains are looking frothy to TD Cowen's David Deckelbaum, who downgraded Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC) to Hold from Buy as shares have breached his price target and net asset value of $5/share, saying the situation "remains iterative, but for now, we see little rationale for a significantly enhanced economic model with current anchor tenant, GM, providing an undisclosed price floor."

However, the analyst believes Lithium Americas (LAC) stands to be a major producer of battery grade lithium carbonate, particularly in North America as Thacker Pass comes online, and will benefit from growing demand for electric vehicles and the general electrification trend.

The recent reports point towards a reassessment of GM's existing offtake agreement with a push for a take-or-pay structure to ensure sales, but Deckelbaum believes the market already had priced in minimally 100% of Phase 1 sales as shares currently imply pricing in excess of $20K/tonne, well above the current ~$10.3k/tonne spot and in light of GM's nearly $950M investment to date.
